flowPage.title: Where does GMV go?

dataQuality.intro: What the dashboard {emph} tell you yet. Every number on the Overview page assumes these warnings are at zero — anything below is a known gap.

summary.unmappedDesc: External SKUs in imports without an internal SKU mapping
summary.missingCogsDesc: Mapped SKUs that lack a cost record — margins are unreliable
summary.unsettledDesc: Orders without a matching settlement row yet
summary.undefinedBundlesDesc: Multi-SKU strings used in mappings that aren't defined as bundles

imports.title: Import history
imports.intro: Every CSV/XLSX upload creates a batch. Deleting a batch cascades to its raw rows and triggers an ETL rebuild for the affected (channel, date range).
imports.deleteConfirm: Delete batch #{id}? Raw rows will be removed and fact_orders will be recomputed for the affected period.

upload.title: Upload
upload.intro: Upload marketplace exports here. Files are previewed before being written — headers are validated against what we expect for the chosen file type.

preview.duplicateTitle: This file has already been imported.
preview.title: Preview
preview.missingColumnsHelp: This usually means the wrong file type was selected. Cancel and try again.

products.title: Products
products.intro: Master product list and current COGS. Click any row to edit.
products.noneYet: No products yet. Insert rows into

bundles.title: Bundles
bundles.intro: Bundle definitions. When a bundle SKU sells, the ETL explodes it into component SKUs for COGS and stock reconciliation. Click any bundle to edit.

skuMappingPage.title: SKU mapping
skuMappingPage.intro: Each channel's "Seller SKU" mapped to our internal SKU. Unmapped rows cause line items to land in fact_orders without a SKU until resolved. Click any row to edit.

product.errSkuAndNameRequired: SKU and product name are required.

bundle.errSkuAndNameRequired: Bundle SKU and name are required.
bundle.errAtLeastOneComponent: At least one component is required.

skuMapping.overrideCogsPlaceholder: Leave blank to use product COGS
skuMapping.errAllRequired: Channel, external SKU, and internal SKU are required.

etl.noImportsYet: No imports yet — nothing to rebuild.

skuSuggester.title: Suggest channel mappings
skuSuggester.intro: Distinct {sellerSku} values from imported sales that don't yet have a mapping. Suggestions are fuzzy-matched against your loaded products. Edit, skip, or confirm.
skuSuggester.savedHint: Re-run the ETL to apply them to existing sales.
skuSuggester.allMapped: All seller_sku values are already mapped. Nothing to suggest.
skuSuggester.saveNothingError: Nothing to save — every row is set to Skip.

products.footer: {n} products. Edit any field — your changes are saved when you click Commit.

adminImportSkus.title: Import SKUs
adminImportSkus.uploadIntro: Upload {file} (RSP + Bundling sheets). The parser auto-generates SKU codes following the {pattern} convention and fuzzy-matches bundle components against your products. Everything is editable in the preview before commit.
adminImportSkus.previewIntro: Review the generated SKUs and bundle component matches. Edit anything that needs correcting, then click Commit. Re-running with the same file is safe — entries upsert.
adminImportSkus.tipPostMap: Tip: after mapping seller SKUs (or any time you change reference data), click {rerunBold} to refresh {factOrders} with the new SKU links and COGS.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.bigPicturePara1: This dashboard answers {bold} It takes raw exports from TikTok Shop (and eventually Shopee, Tokopedia), combines them with your product master and SKU mapping, and computes per-line-item economics — gross GMV minus platform fees, discounts, vouchers, affiliate commissions, ads, and COGS — to give you a contribution margin for every order line.
howItWorks.bigPictureBold: "which products and channels actually make money?"
howItWorks.bigPicturePara2: The output is sliced by {bold}, so you can see not just "TikTok did 200M GMV" but "Combo Glowing Jumbo on TikTok via creator X had a 12% margin while via creator Y had a 3% margin."
howItWorks.step1Para1Pre: The system needs to know your products before it can analyse sales. Upload the
howItWorks.step1Para1Mid: file (RSP sheet + Bundling sheet) on the
howItWorks.step1L1: Auto-generates SKU codes (e.g. {code} for Soothing Baby Creme 70gr)
howItWorks.step1L2: Reads HPP into {code} (used for margin calculation)
howItWorks.step1L3: Fuzzy-matches bundle components to your products — you confirm or override
howItWorks.step1L4: Skips gifts/free items so they don't double-count in COGS
howItWorks.step1Para2: You can re-upload to update — entries upsert, so it's safe.
howItWorks.step2Para1Post: and pick the channel + file type. For TikTok Shop, the dashboard expects five exports (each from a different page in Seller Center / Ads Manager):

step2Files.incomeDesc: Finance → Statements → Export (xlsx). Has fee breakdown + settlement amounts.
step2Files.ordersDesc: Orders → Export (csv). Has the SKU codes (Seller SKU) you sold.
step2Files.returnsDesc: After-Sales → Export. Has return reasons and refund amounts.
step2Files.affiliateDesc: Affiliate → Performance → Export. Maps orders to creators.
step2Files.adsDesc: TikTok Ads Manager → Campaigns → Export. NOT in Seller Center — separate tool.

howItWorks.step2Para2: The uploader validates headers, previews the data, and lets you confirm before it writes anything. Files are deduplicated by content hash, so re-uploading the same file is harmless.
howItWorks.step3Title: Link external SKUs to your internal SKUs
howItWorks.step3Tag: after first upload, plus when new SKUs appear
howItWorks.step3Para1: TikTok stores your SKU as whatever you typed into Seller Center — often a slightly different format from your canonical code (e.g. {code1} vs your {code2}) or a space-separated bundle string like {code3}.
howItWorks.step3Para2Post: and click {bold}. The system pulls every distinct seller_sku that doesn't yet have a mapping, fuzzy-matches it against your products, and shows you confidence levels. Confirm or override, then save.
howItWorks.step3Para3: Until a seller_sku is mapped, the corresponding sales rows have {code} and aren't included in product-level analytics. The {link} page lists unmapped SKUs and how much GMV they represent.
howItWorks.step4Title: Re-run ETL when reference data changes
howItWorks.step4Para1: The ETL runs automatically right after every sales upload. But when you change {bold1} — new SKU mappings, updated COGS, edited bundle components — the existing sales rows still hold the old computed values. Click {bold2} on the SKU Mapping page (or anywhere it appears) to recompute {code} for the full imported period.
howItWorks.step4Para2: It's idempotent — running it twice gives the same result. The button auto-detects channels and date ranges from your import history; you don't need to pick anything.
howItWorks.behindIntro: Behind every upload, this pipeline runs:
howItWorks.behindStep1Body: The file is parsed (CSV or XLSX), headers are checked against what's expected for that channel + file type, and a hash of the file content is computed for deduplication. You see a preview before anything writes to the database.
howItWorks.behindStep2Body: One table per (channel, file type) — e.g. {code1}, {code2}. Each row is tied to an {code3} entry so you can audit and roll back.
howItWorks.behindStep3Body: The ETL joins raw orders + settlements + your SKU mapping + product COGS into {code}: one row per order line, with revenue decomposed into gross GMV, discounts, fees, affiliate commission, COGS, packaging, ads, and a final contribution margin. Bundles get their COGS summed from components automatically.
howItWorks.behindStep4Body: All charts read from {code} and a handful of views built on top of it. Nothing is recalculated at view time — it's all pre-computed.
howItWorks.behindFootnote: Files are kept in Supabase Storage at {path} for traceability. Deleting an import batch cascades to its raw rows and re-runs ETL for the affected period.

tiles.overviewDesc: Top-line GMV, contribution margin, and trend. Your daily check-in.
tiles.channelsDesc: TikTok-specific: take rate, fee waterfall (where the money leaks), affiliate burden. Shopee/Tokopedia coming as data flows in.
tiles.skusDesc: Margin per product per channel. Find your stars and your bleeders.
tiles.flowDesc: Sankey diagram of GMV → cost of revenue → net settlement. Visual fee decomposition.
tiles.dqDesc: What's incomplete: unmapped SKUs, missing COGS, undefined bundles, unsettled orders. Fix from here.
tiles.importsDesc: Every file you've uploaded, with row counts and date coverage. Delete a batch here to roll back its data.

howItWorks.wf1Q: "I just got new TikTok exports — what do I do?"
howItWorks.wf1A: {uploadLink} each file (orders, settlements, returns, affiliate, ads). The ETL runs automatically after each one. Then check the {overviewLink} and {skusLink} pages. If the {dqLink} page flags new unmapped SKUs, head to step 3 above.
howItWorks.wf2Q: "I added or changed a product in our SKU master."
howItWorks.wf2A: Re-upload the {masterLink} file. Then click {bold} on {mapLink} so historical sales pick up the new COGS / bundle definitions.
howItWorks.wf3Q: "Some sales show no SKU on the dashboard."
howItWorks.wf3A: That means the seller_sku from TikTok hasn't been mapped to an internal SKU yet. Go to {mapLink}, click {suggestBold}, confirm the matches, then {rerunBold}.
howItWorks.wf4A: Go to {importsLink} and delete the batch. Raw rows cascade-delete and the ETL automatically rebuilds for the affected period.

glossary.gmv: Gross Merchandise Value — total order amount before any discounts or fees.
glossary.hpp: Cost of Goods Sold — what each unit costs you to make. Stored per-SKU and time-versioned.
glossary.takeRate: The percentage of GMV that the platform keeps (commission + fees + discounts you fund).
glossary.netSettlement: What the platform actually pays you after all platform-side deductions.
glossary.cm: Net settlement minus COGS, packaging, and attributed ad spend. The bottom-line profit per line item.
glossary.bundle: A SKU that ships as multiple component SKUs. COGS is summed from components automatically.
glossary.extInt: External = whatever the channel stores (e.g. TikTok's seller_sku). Internal = your canonical PUR-* code.
glossary.factRaw: Raw = exact mirror of the export file. Fact = channel-agnostic, line-item, with COGS and margin computed.
